key_insight: GNDEs can be trained on a small sparse graph and then transferred zero-shot to larger graphon-sampled graphs, with provable convergence of both forward dynamics and adjoint gradients.
engineering_relevance: This gives a theoretical basis for scalable graph dynamics models that avoid retraining when moving to larger system sizes, which is useful for simulation, control, and learning on growing networks.
method: Model GNDEs and their infinite-node counterparts as Graphon-NDEs and adjoint Graphon-NDEs.
Prove trajectory-wise and uniform-in-time convergence on sparse random graphs with rates depending on sparsity and graph size.
Analyze DTO vs OTD training under explicit Euler discretization and derive asymptotic consistency bounds.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports experiments on HSBM and tent graphons, plus zero-shot transfer tests across four graphon classes, showing accurate deployment on larger independently sampled graphs.
watch_item: The guarantees depend on graphon sampling assumptions, sparsity scaling, and explicit Euler discretization, so practical performance may weaken if the deployment graph deviates from these conditions.
paper_title: Zero-Shot Size Transfer for Neural ODEs on Sparse Random Graphs: Graphon Limits and Adjoint Convergence
